pairs.question: Who is the head coach of the University of Pennsylvania lightweight rowing team?
pairs.answer: Colin Farrell is the head coach of the University of Pennsylvania lightweight rowing team.

pairs.question: What significant rowing achievement did Colin Farrell accomplish in 2008?
pairs.answer: In 2008, Colin Farrell won the World Rowing Championships in the lightweight men's eight.

pairs.question: What are some of Colin Farrell's notable accomplishments as a collegiate rower at Cornell University?
pairs.answer: As a rower at Cornell, Colin Farrell was a three-time letter winner, two-time captain, helped the team win a silver medal at the 2005 IRA Regatta, and stroked the Big Red to a 3rd-place finish in the Men's Lightweight Eight at the 2005 Eastern Sprints.

pairs.question: When did Colin Farrell begin serving as the head coach of the University of Pennsylvania lightweight rowing team?
pairs.answer: Colin Farrell has been serving as the head coach of the Pennsylvania lightweight rowing team since 2014.

pairs.question: What historic event did the University of Pennsylvania lightweight rowing team achieve under Colin Farrell’s leadership in 2019?
pairs.answer: Under Farrell’s leadership in 2019, the University of Pennsylvania lightweight rowing team brought an Eight and a Four to the Henley Royal Regatta, competing in the Temple Challenge Cup and Prince Albert Challenge Cup, respectively. This marked the first appearance of Penn lightweights at Henley since 1991.

pairs.question: What recent success has the Penn Varsity Eight achieved under Colin Farrell’s coaching?
pairs.answer: The Penn Varsity Eight has medaled at the Eastern Sprints for the past three years, including winning the event in 2019.

pairs.question: Where does Colin Farrell reside, and what notable personal achievement did he receive in 2015?
pairs.answer: Colin Farrell resides in Moorestown, New Jersey, with his wife and two sons. In 2015, he was elected to the Cornell Athletics Hall of Fame.
